title: Kaju Katli (Cashew Fudge)

ingredients.title: Ingredients
items: Cashews (Kaju): 250 g (use fresh, unsalted, premium-grade whole cashews)
Edible silver leaf (Vark): 2 sheets (optional, for garnish)
Rose water or Kewra water: ¼ tsp (optional, for fragrance)

instruction.title: Preparing Cashew Powder
steps: Place cashews in the freezer for 30 minutes (this prevents oil release during grinding).
Grind cashews in a high-power blender or spice grinder to a fine powder.
Do not over-grind; it can turn into cashew butter.
Sift once to remove any larger bits.

instruction.title: Sugar Syrup
steps: In a heavy-bottom pan, add sugar and water.
Heat gently, stirring until sugar dissolves.
Boil until it reaches one-string consistency (test: stretch syrup between thumb and forefinger — a thin single thread should form).

instruction.title: Making the Dough
steps: Reduce flame to low. Add cashew powder gradually into the syrup.
Stir continuously using a flat spatula, ensuring no lumps.
Cook until the mixture thickens and starts leaving the pan (approx. 8–10 minutes).

instruction.title: Shaping
steps: Grease a smooth surface (steel tray or marble slab) with ghee.
Transfer the hot mixture, let it cool for 1–2 minutes until warm but touchable.
Grease palms lightly with ghee and knead into a soft, smooth dough (like roti dough).
Place between two sheets of parchment and roll evenly to 3–5 mm thickness.
Cut into diamond-shaped pieces with a sharp knife.

chefTips: Cashew Quality: Always use fresh cashews — old ones give a bitter aftertaste.
Syrup Consistency: Too thin → mixture won’t set; too thick → dough becomes hard.
Rolling: Work quickly while warm; once cool, it hardens and cracks.
Luxury Twist: Add a pinch of saffron or cardamom powder to the syrup for a premium restaurant flavor.
